title: Harden the runner and bootstrap: absolute stop path, iteration timeout, single-instance guard
action:
Three review findings, all in the scripts.

(1) Both stop-file paths are RELATIVE to the invoker's cwd. Bootstrap claims to refuse on a stale stop file, but it runs from wherever the human is while the loop runs from --main-root - so they check different files, and bootstrap can report 'dispatch loop started' for a loop that exits on its first iteration. Resolve the stop file against the main checkout in both scripts (control-loop.sh can take --main-root, or resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`), so both always mean the same file.

(2) A hung iteration makes the human's stop gesture unreachable: the stop file is only read at the top of an iteration, and `claude -p` runs with no timeout, so a stalled invocation blocks forever and the only recourse is killing the pane. Wrap the invocation in a timeout (default 900s, overridable), and treat a timeout as an ordinary failed iteration - reported, loop continues (D19).

(3) bootstrap-cockpit.sh is not idempotent: run twice, or after a herdr restart, and it creates a second cockpit and a second dispatch loop polling the same backlog - two loops can both pass the no-worktree-grant check in the same second and both spawn. Refuse when a pane labelled `dispatch` already exists in the target workspace, naming it.

Keep all copies byte-identical, and do not disturb the loop's no-exit-on-error property or the stop-file check itself - both are proven and load-bearing.

truths: The stop file resolves to the same absolute path whether bootstrap or the loop is asked
A hung iteration is bounded by a timeout and reported as a failed iteration, and the loop continues
Running bootstrap when a dispatch pane already exists refuses instead of creating a second loop
Three consecutive failing iterations still all run (the D19 property is untouched)
All four copies are byte-identical in their pairs

artifacts.substantive: absolute stop path and a bounded iteration

prohibitions: Do not add a retry around a failed iteration; report and continue is the contract (D19)
Do not remove or weaken the stop-file check
Do not change the claude model or permission flags

verify: set -e; S=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; B=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/bootstrap-cockpit.sh; diff -q "$S" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; diff -q "$B" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/bootstrap-cockpit.sh; grep -qE 'timeout' "$S"; grep -qE 'show-toplevel|--main-root' "$S"; grep -qi 'dispatch' "$B"; mkdir -p .bee/tmp; rm -f .bee/tmp/apo-h.log .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop; bash "$S" --role dispatch --interval 1 --max-iterations 3 --command "echo x >> .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; exit 1" || true; test "$(wc -l < .bee/tmp/apo-h.log)" = 3; rm -f .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; touch .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop; bash "$S" --role dispatch --interval 1 --max-iterations 3 --command "echo x >> .bee/tmp/apo-h.log" || true; test ! -s .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; rm -f .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; echo RUNNER_HARDENED

trace.outcome: A stop gesture that means the same file everywhere, an iteration that cannot hang forever, and a bootstrap that refuses to start a second loop against the same backlog.
trace.verify_output:
interval=1s
control-loop.sh: iteration failed with exit code 1; continuing
control-loop.sh: iteration failed with exit code 1; continuing
control-loop.sh: iteration failed with exit code 1; continuing
interval=1s
control-loop.sh: stop file found at /home/vantt/projects/herdr-gateway--wt--agent-pane-orchestration/.bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop; exiting
RUNNER_HARDENED
trace.verify_command: set -e; S=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; B=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/bootstrap-cockpit.sh; diff -q "$S" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh; diff -q "$B" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/scripts/bootstrap-cockpit.sh; grep -qE 'timeout' "$S"; grep -qE 'show-toplevel|--main-root' "$S"; grep -qi 'dispatch' "$B"; mkdir -p .bee/tmp; rm -f .bee/tmp/apo-h.log .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop; bash "$S" --role dispatch --interval 1 --max-iterations 3 --command "echo x >> .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; exit 1" || true; test "$(wc -l < .bee/tmp/apo-h.log)" = 3; rm -f .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; touch .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop; bash "$S" --role dispatch --interval 1 --max-iterations 3 --command "echo x >> .bee/tmp/apo-h.log" || true; test ! -s .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; rm -f .bee/tmp/herdr-orchestrating.stop .bee/tmp/apo-h.log; echo RUNNER_HARDENED
